hypotheses.name: Joint 6D (SV2-7) TF/target classifier cross-seed validation
hypotheses.method: For each of 3 seeds (main, seed43, seed44): SVD of centered nonzero embeddings at 12 layers. Project genes to SV2-4 and SV5-7, concatenate to 6D. 5-fold stratified LR, mean AUROC. 50-perm label-shuffle null. TF (n~70-73) vs target-only (n~211-222).
hypotheses.result_value: 0.751 (main=0.744, seed43=0.753, seed44=0.757); all seeds p_perm=0.000 at all 12 layers
hypotheses.next_action: Compute cross-seed TF/target boundary consistency (do same TF genes rank highest across seeds?); test generalization to held-out gene sets

hypotheses.name: Seed43 SV basis alignment via principal angles
hypotheses.method: For each layer/subspace (SV2-4, SV5-7): compute principal angles between right singular vectors (512-dim feature space) of main vs seed43 and seed44. Also test main SV2-4 vs seed43 SV5-7 for basis permutation hypothesis.
hypotheses.result_value: mean PA(sv57, main vs s43)=29.0°, PA(main vs s44)=19.6°; cross-subspace PA=74.8° (near-orthogonal, rules out permutation)
hypotheses.next_action: Retire basis-permutation hypothesis. Focus on combined-subspace (6D span) stability rather than individual SV alignment.
hypotheses.retirement_reason: Basis permutation ruled out; SV subspace instability is real but does not explain cross-seed AUROC patterns

hypotheses.name: SV energy fraction as AUROC proxy
hypotheses.method: For main seed, compute variance fraction of SV groups (SV1, SV2-4, SV5-7, SV2-7) at each of 12 layers. Spearman correlation between SV energy fractions and joint/subspace AUROC across layers.
hypotheses.result_value: rho=-0.930, p=0.00001; early layers have lower joint SV fraction but higher AUROC
hypotheses.next_action: Interpret as geometry descriptor: TF/target discrimination is highest when variance is spread across many SVs (richer geometry). Report as structural property. Test in cross-seed.
